sourceMovie.title: See No Evil
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: See No Evil works as a thriller movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The thriller and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Don't Breathe
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If See No Evil clicked for you, Don't Breathe (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after See No Evil?
faq.answer: Try Don't Breathe, Beast, Possessor—each shares DNA with See No Evil while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is See No Evil?
faq.answer: See No Evil (1971) is best described as Thriller, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is See No Evil worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
